# Set Operations

Set objects also support mathematical operations like union, intersection, difference, and symmetric difference.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/b246a55f-c34b-4e7f-aa1b-9ae1095274e6.png)

## Union

Union of two sets is a set containing all elements of both sets.

`set_a | set_b`
or
`set_a.union(sequence)`

`union()` converts sequence to a set, and performs the union.  

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
set_b = {1, 2}
union = set_a | set_b
print(union)
```

#### Output

```

{1, 2, 4, 8}
```

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
list_a = [1, 2]
union = set_a.union(list_a)
print(union)
```

#### Output

```

{1, 2, 4, 8}
```

## Intersection

Intersection of two sets is a set containing common elements of both sets.

`set_a & set_b` 
or
`set_a.intersection(sequence)`

`intersection()` converts sequence to a set, and perform the intersection. 

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
set_b = {1, 2}
intersection = set_a & set_b
print(intersection)
```

#### Output

```

{2}
```

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
list_a = [1, 2]

intersection = set_a.intersection(list_a)
print(intersection)
```

#### Output

```

{2}
```

## Difference

Difference of two sets is a set containing all the elements in the first set but not second.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/1c502d4f-a4d0-496c-b77b-1dc080ea2ef1.png)

` set_a - set_b`
or
`set_a.difference(sequence)`

`difference()` converts sequence to a set.

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
set_b = {1, 2}
diff = set_a - set_b
print(diff)
```

#### Output

```

{8, 4}
```

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
tuple_a = (1, 2)
diff = set_a.difference(tuple_a)
print(diff)
```

#### Output

```

{8, 4}
```

## Symmetric Difference

Symmetric difference of two sets is a set containing all elements which are not common to both sets.

`set_a ^ set_b`
or
`set_a.symmetric_difference(sequence)`

`symmetric_difference()` converts sequence to a set.

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
set_b = {1, 2}
symmetric_diff = set_a ^ set_b
print(symmetric_diff)
```

#### Output

```

{8, 1, 4}
```

#### Code

```python
set_a = {4, 2, 8}
set_b = {1, 2}
diff = set_a.symmetric_difference(set_b)
print(diff)
```

#### Output

```

{8, 1, 4}
```

# Set Comparisons

Set comparisons are used to validate whether one set fully exists within another

-   issubset()
-   issuperset()
-   isdisjoint()

## Subset

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/76c1b6db-9207-40c5-bbb9-a4859f2c9716.png)

`set2.issubset(set1)` Returns `True` if all elements of second set are in first set. Else, `False`

*Example - 1*
  

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/008e7d8a-1cff-4e08-89e5-58a703e8b1f4.png)

#### Code

```python
set_1 = {'a', 1, 3, 5}
set_2 = {'a', 1}
is_subset = set_2.issubset(set_1)
print(is_subset)
```

#### Output

```

True
```


*Example - 2*

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/245a5fd8-be29-4d38-a7e9-18072e3e2f64.png)


#### Code

```python
set_1 = {4, 6}
set_2 = {2, 6}
is_subset = set_2.issubset(set_1)
print(is_subset)
```

#### Output

```

False
```

## SuperSet

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/c1428abd-455d-4a22-b54c-9b5427ff51dd.png)

`set1.issuperset(set2)` Returns `True` if all elements of second set are in first set. Else, `False`


*Example - 1*

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/1c5d388b-f7c2-4f31-820c-f8ef16d3bd43.png)


#### Code

```python
set_1 = {'a', 1, 3, 5}
set_2 = {'a', 1}
is_superset = set_1.issuperset(set_2)
print(is_superset)
```

#### Output

```

True
```
  

*Example - 2*

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/4c705974-c8b9-40d6-b540-52cbce82396a.png)


#### Code

```python
set_1 = {4, 6}
set_2 = {2, 6}
is_superset = set_1.issuperset(set_2)
print(is_superset)
```

#### Output

```

False
```

## Disjoint Sets

`set1.isdisjoint(set2)` Returns `True` when they have no common elements. Else, `False`

#### Code

```python
set_a = {1, 2}
set_b = {3, 4}
is_disjoint = set_a.isdisjoint(set_b)
print(is_disjoint)
```

#### Output

```

True

```
